meta.title: Bamboo Money — Your money, growing.
meta.description: Bamboo Money brings your spending, subscriptions, and net worth into one calm, intelligent space — so you can focus on building the life you want.

hero.subheadline: Bamboo Money brings your spending, subscriptions, and net worth into one calm, intelligent space — so you can focus on building the life you want.

problem.label: The problem
problem.headline: Managing money shouldn't feel like this.

budgeting.title: Budgeting feels like homework
budgeting.body: 60% of users abandon budgeting apps within 90 days. The tools demand too much work for too little payoff.

dashboards.title: Dashboards show everything but change nothing
dashboards.body: Knowing you spent $847 on dining last month doesn't help you spend less this month. Insight without action is just noise.

fragmented.title: Five different apps for one financial life
fragmented.body: Budget in one app, subscriptions in another, net worth in a third. Your financial picture is scattered across tools that don't talk to each other.

pillars.label: How it works
pillars.headline: One app. Three pillars. Your whole financial picture.

envelopes.title: Envelopes
envelopes.body: Know exactly what you have left to spend — in real time, for every category. Not after the month ends. Right now.

subscriptions.title: Subscriptions
subscriptions.body: Every recurring charge, detected and tracked. See what you're paying, what you're not using, and what that money could do instead.

netWorth.title: Net Worth
netWorth.body: All your accounts — checking, savings, investments, debt — in one quiet number that grows over time.

pillars.bridge: And an intelligent assistant that ties it all together.

assistant.label: Meet Bamboo
assistant.headline: Not a chatbot. An intelligence woven into everything.
assistant.description: Bamboo observes your financial patterns and delivers insights you can act on — calm, specific, and grounded in your actual data.
insights: Your grocery envelope has $62 remaining this week. Past months show this is where spending spikes — a good moment to plan ahead.
Three subscriptions haven't been used in 60+ days. Canceling all three adds $912/year to your travel fund.
Your net worth has grown $2,340 in the last 90 days. At this rate, you'll reach your $50K milestone in November.

vsYnab.label: vs. YNAB
vsYnab.text: YNAB gives every dollar a job. Bamboo gives your dollars jobs for you — based on the life you told us you want.

vsMonarch.label: vs. Monarch
vsMonarch.text: Monarch shows you everything. Bamboo shows you what to do about it.

vsRocket.label: vs. Rocket Money
vsRocket.text: Rocket Money cleans up your past. Bamboo builds your future.

couples.title: Couples merging finances
couples.body: Shared clarity without shared stress. See where your money goes together.

quitters.title: People who've tried and quit
quitters.body: Budgeting shouldn't feel like a second job. Bamboo does the work for you.

goalSetters.title: Goal-setters
goalSetters.body: Connect daily spending to your dream life — travel, a home, freedom from debt.

bilingual.title: English & Spanish
bilingual.body: Available in both languages. Financial tools should meet you where you are.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.headline: Financial clarity should be accessible.
pricing.footnote: Less than $1 per week. Less than half the cost of Monarch or YNAB.

cta.headline: Start growing your money.
cta.description: Join the waitlist for early access. Be among the first to experience a calmer way to manage your finances.

ask.title: Ask Bamboo
suggestions: How did we do this month?
Am I on track for my goals?

response.title: March Summary (through day 9)
response.overall: Overall: You're tracking well. The main thing to watch is dining — if the pace holds, you'll go $66 over by month end. Cooking three more meals at home this month would close that gap.
followUps: How does this compare to Feb?

emergency.name: Emergency Fund
emergency.insight: At current pace, you'll reach 3 months of expenses by August. Half a safety net is still a safety net.

japan.name: Japan Trip
japan.insight: You're 45% of the way there. At this pace, you'll have enough for flights and two weeks of travel by fall.

creditCard.name: Credit Card Freedom
creditCard.insight: You've eliminated $3,800 in debt since you started. That's $3,800 that isn't accruing interest anymore.

insights.todayInsight: Day 9 of March. Four of six envelopes are on pace. Dining is running 20% ahead of last month — worth watching.
insights.groceryInsight: Your grocery spending averaged $187/week this month — $22 above your envelope target. The increase is concentrated on weekends.
insights.subscriptionInsight: Three recurring charges haven't been used in 60+ days:
insights.groceryFollowUp: How can I reduce weekend grocery spending?
